calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-17-2026 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-04-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-17-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-02-2025 Continued To Dec-15-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Rahman Popal, Esq. 628-200-3018 The Law Firm for Tenant Rights for Plaintiff.
Catherine Song, Esq. 415-981-5411 Bledsoe Dietsel Treppa and Crane LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED. Ms. Song contests the tentative ruling re: sanctions and presents arguments. The Court comments and poses questions to both sides re sanctions of $4500. Mr. Popar responds.
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFs NETSANET ABRAHA,and ROEBEL HOUSTON's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs Netsanet Abraha and Roebel Houston's motion to compel further responses to discovery requests is granted.

Preliminarily, Plaintiffs are advised that stacked discovery motions are disfavored. Stacked motions may be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frogs, SRogs, RFPs, RFAs, deposition notice) addressed to a single, specific party.

The motion is granted as to Plaintiffs' Special Interrogatories, Set One, Nos. 24-27, 40, and 41. The interrogatories at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiffs have shown Defendants Samuel Luk, Catherine Luk and Louisa Luk's responses are evasive/incomplete. Defendants have not demonstrated that any objection has merit. Defendants fail to show that the information is equally available to Plaintiffs. Persons other than Plaintiffs (e.g. other tenants) could have made complaints and Plaintiffs are not necessarily aware of all remedial measures taken. Plaintiffs' meet and confer was reasonable. The court orders Defendants to provide further responses to the cited special interrogatories. Defendants must provide verified responses no later than 10 court days from notice of this order.

The motion is granted as to Request for Production of Documents, Set One, Nos. 52-54, 57. The requests are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiffs have shown Defendants' responses are evasive/incomplete. Defendants have not demonstrated that any objection has merit. Plaintiffs' meet and confer was reasonable. The court orders Defendants to provide further verified production no later than 10 court days from notice of this order.

Defendants have not shown their actions in connection with this discovery and motion were substantially justified. Defendants are ordered to pay Plaintiffs $3,200 in sanctions, payable to Plaintiffs' counsel by May 2, 2025. The court rejects Defendants' notice argument, at least as to sanctions against Defendants (as opposed to counsel). Plaintiffs requested sanctions against Defendants in the caption of their motion and the body of their notice of motion. In any event, Defendants responded on the merits as to sanctions (even seeking sanctions for themselves in the same amount), thus there is no prejudice.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
